---
pagetitle: "SQLTok: a schema token budget manager for Text-to-SQL"
description: "Select only the schema that matters, within a hard token budget, with guaranteed-joinable tables."
toc: false
---

::: {.hero}
![](assets/logo.png){fig-alt="SQLTok logo"}

# SQLTok

::: {.tagline}
A schema token budget manager for Text-to-SQL.
Send less schema, write better SQL, pay fewer tokens.
:::

::: {.badge-row}
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/badge/pip%20install-sqltok-blue.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/sqltok/)
[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://github.com/ShovalBenjer/sqltok/blob/main/LICENSE)
[![GitHub](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-sqltok-181717.svg?logo=github)](https://github.com/ShovalBenjer/sqltok)
:::
:::

## Measured on BIRD mini-dev

Across all 500 BIRD mini-dev questions over 11 databases, with a 1000-token budget:

::: {layout-ncol=3 .text-center}
::: {.metric}
::: {.num}
57%
:::
::: {.lbl}
fewer schema tokens
:::
:::

::: {.metric}
::: {.num}
53%
:::
::: {.lbl}
fewer total input tokens
:::
:::

::: {.metric}
::: {.num}
0
:::
::: {.lbl}
network calls in the core
:::
:::
:::

These figures are deterministic and need no model. Execution accuracy is scored with the official BIRD script.

## What it does

When a language model writes SQL from a question, it needs the schema in the prompt. Sending the whole schema is expensive and lowers accuracy. SQLTok selects only the relevant tables and columns within a configurable token budget, measures the result with `tiktoken`, and guarantees the selected tables are connected by foreign keys so the model can write valid joins.

```python
from sqltok import SchemaBudgetManager

mgr = SchemaBudgetManager.from_sqlite("northwind.sqlite")
ctx = mgr.build_context("total order amount for customers in France", token_budget=2000)

print(ctx.text)        # compact CREATE TABLE schema, at most 2000 tokens
print(ctx.tables)      # ['customers', 'orders']
print(ctx.token_count) # measured, guaranteed at or below the budget
```

## How it works

Three working stages and a guarantee. Read the full, illustrated walkthrough in [the visual guide](posts/visual-guide-to-sqltok/index.qmd).

![](assets/diagrams/pipeline.svg){fig-alt="SQLTok pipeline: grounding, coverage, foreign-key Steiner connectivity, and a measured budget"}

1. **Grounding** maps question mentions to tables, columns, and sampled cell values using a native MinHash and LSH index.
2. **Coverage** selects tables by submodular maximum coverage under the token budget, with a `(1 - 1/e)` guarantee.
3. **Foreign-key Steiner connectivity** adds the minimal bridge tables so the selection is joinable.

## Install

```bash
pip install sqltok
```

[Read the visual guide](posts/visual-guide-to-sqltok/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary} &nbsp;
[View on GitHub](https://github.com/ShovalBenjer/sqltok){.btn .btn-outline-light}
